title: Questcast Game State
description: The JSON structure injected into the system prompt as {game_state_json}. This is the contract between the Backend and the AI prompt layer. Only fields relevant to the current context should be included to save tokens.

session_id.description: Unique session identifier. Used for save/load and analytics.

character.description: The player's character. The AI uses this to personalize narration, reference abilities, and track health.

name.description: Character's name. AI should use this in narration and NPC dialogue.

class.description: Character class. Affects available abilities and how the AI describes the character's actions.

level.description: Character level. Higher levels = the AI can present tougher challenges.

health.description: Current HP. At 0, AI triggers death saves. Low HP should increase narration tension.

max_health.description: Maximum HP. Used to calculate health percentage for tension scaling.

inventory.description: Items the character carries. AI can reference these in narration and suggest their use.

gold.description: Gold pieces. Relevant for merchant NPCs and bribery options.

abilities.description: Special abilities available. AI should occasionally hint the player can use these.

story.description: Story progression state. The AI uses this to maintain narrative coherence across turns and sessions.

current_chapter.description: Current story chapter. Affects narrative scope and available locations.

current_location.description: Where the player is right now. AI uses this for scene descriptions and available actions.

active_quest.description: The main objective. AI should reference this to keep the player on track.

quest_progress.description: Progress toward quest completion (0.0 to 1.0). AI uses this to pace revelations and escalate stakes.

npcs_met.description: NPCs the player has encountered. AI should remember these and reference past interactions.

key_decisions.description: Important choices the player made. AI should reference consequences of these decisions.

narrative_summary.description: Rolling 10-turn narrative summary generated by conversation_summary.txt. This is the AI's 'long-term memory' of the adventure.

world.description: Environmental state. Affects scene descriptions, NPC behavior, and encounter difficulty.

time_of_day.description: Current time. Affects lighting, NPC availability, and creature encounters.

weather.description: Current weather. AI uses this for atmospheric details in scene descriptions.

threat_level.description: Current danger level. AI scales tension, encounter frequency, and narration urgency accordingly.

session.description: Session metadata. Used for pacing, limits, and save triggers.

turns_played.description: Total turns this session. At 10-turn intervals, triggers conversation summary. At max (60), triggers session end.

images_generated.description: Images generated this session. Maximum 2. AI should suggest image-worthy moments only when budget remains.

time_elapsed_minutes.description: Minutes played. Soft limit at 45 (AI hints at stopping), hard limit at 60 (triggers cliffhanger).

last_saved_at.description: Timestamp of last save. Auto-save triggers every 5 turns.

token_optimization_notes.description: To minimize token usage, the backend SHOULD:
rules: Omit empty arrays (e.g., inventory: [] -> omit entirely)
Omit session metadata unless near limits (turns > 50 or time > 40 min)
Omit images_generated unless the backend is considering an image trigger
Use short location names (e.g., 'Dark Forest' not 'The Ancient and Mysterious Dark Forest of Shadows')
Truncate narrative_summary if it exceeds 120 tokens
